data.title: Coffee, Cold Hands, Cathedrals
data.excerpt: The alarm goes off at 3am and the ritual begins. A short meditation on what happens in the dark hour before an alpine start.
data.content:
![Blue enamel coffee mug on weathered wooden table at dawn, soft window light](/uploads/blog/a-steaming-enamel-mug-of-coffee-sitting--n0ry.jpg)

The alarm is set for 3am. You don't really sleep before it — you just wait.

The ritual is the same every time. Headlamp on, stove lit, enamel mug filled. You move quietly around the hut like you're trying not to wake the mountain. You probably are.

Cold hands wrap around hot metal. The coffee is bad and it's perfect. Outside, the sky is still fully committed to night.

This is the part nobody photographs. Not the summit, not the sunrise — the ten minutes before you lace your boots, when the plan is still intact and everything feels possible. The cathedral hasn't opened yet, but you're already on the steps.

You finish the coffee. You check your kit one more time. You open the door.

The cold hits you like a handshake.

Let's go.
data.author: Agentic Tester
